blocks.body: Present Continuous — actions happening now, temporary situations, and words like now or at the moment.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"Present Continuous is for actions happening now, around the present time, or temporarily.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the pattern am / is / are + verb-ing.","example":"I am reading. / She is working."},{"label":"Negatives and Questions","color":"blue","text":"For negatives and questions, work through the form of be.","example":"He isn't sleeping. / Are they coming?"},{"label":"Time expressions","color":"amber","text":"Words like now, right now, at the moment, today, this week often point to this tense.","example":"We are studying now. / She is staying with friends this week."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions. Each card includes short rules from the matching lesson.","items":[{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"am / is / are + verb-ing and the main uses.","rules":[{"label":"Form","formula":"am / is / are + V-ing","text":"Use am / is / are plus the -ing form of the verb.","example":"I am working. / She is reading."},{"label":"Use","formula":"now / temporary","text":"Use it for an action now or a temporary situation around now.","example":"They are studying this week."}]},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"am not, isn't, aren't, and common mistakes with be and -ing.","rules":[{"label":"Negative","formula":"be + not + V-ing","text":"Make negatives with am not / isn't / aren't.","example":"I am not working. / He isn't sleeping."},{"label":"Keep be","formula":"not + V-ing","text":"Do not drop be; it carries the continuous form.","example":"They aren't watching TV."}]},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Am I...?, Is she...?, Are they...? and short answers.","rules":[{"label":"Question","formula":"Am / Is / Are + subject + V-ing?","text":"Put Am / Is / Are at the beginning of the question.","example":"Are you working now? / Is she coming?"},{"label":"Short answers","formula":"be / be not","text":"Repeat the form of be in short answers.","example":"Yes, I am. / No, he isn't."}]},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"now, right now, at the moment, today, this week.","rules":[{"label":"Right now","formula":"now / at the moment","text":"These markers often show an action happening now.","example":"She is talking at the moment."},{"label":"Temporary period","formula":"today / this week","text":"Temporary periods also often need Present Continuous.","example":"We are working from home this week."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Where we use this tense","sections":[{"label":"Now","color":"emerald","description":"Show an action happening at the moment of speaking.","examples":[{"en":"I am talking on the phone now.","ua":"I am talking on the phone now."}]},{"label":"Around the present time","color":"blue","description":"Use the tense for an action happening in the current period, not only this second.","examples":[{"en":"She is reading a new book this week.","ua":"She is reading a new book this week."}]},{"label":"Temporary situations","color":"amber","description":"Show a situation that is not permanent.","examples":[{"en":"They are staying with friends this month.","ua":"They are staying with friends this month."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Present Continuous = am / is / are + verb-ing.","Use this tense for actions happening now or temporarily.","For negatives and questions, the form of be changes, not the main verb.","Time expressions like now or this week often point to this tense."]}
